hook: One of America's most famous airlines, known for its sleek planes, Jet Age glamour, and Howard Hughes, before going bankrupt and being absorbed by American Airlines in 2001.
fate: TWA went bankrupt and American Airlines announced in January 2001 that it would buy most of the airline's assets. TWA stopped flying as its own airline later that year.
summary: TWA stood for Trans World Airlines. It started in 1930 as Transcontinental and Western Air and became one of the most well-known airlines in the United States. It was famous for its long-distance flights, stylish look, and its connection to billionaire Howard Hughes. The airline also flew one of the most beautiful planes of its time, the Lockheed Constellation. TWA went bankrupt and American Airlines bought most of its assets in 2001, ending the airline for good.

seo.meta_title: What Happened to TWA? The Glamorous Airline That Disappeared
seo.meta_description: TWA was one of America's most famous airlines, linked to Howard Hughes, the Constellation era, and the Jet Age. Learn how bankruptcy and American's 2001 asset purchase ended TWA as an independent carrier.

notable_products: Domestic flights connecting cities across the United States, including coast-to-coast routes
International flights including transatlantic service to Europe
The Lockheed Constellation, one of the most recognizable passenger planes of the 1940s and 1950s
The TWA Flight Center at JFK Airport (1962), a famous building designed by architect Eero Saarinen that became the TWA Hotel in 2019
verified_facts: TWA was formed on July 16, 1930 through the amalgamation of divisions of Western Air Express and Transcontinental Air Transport.
The airline originally operated as Transcontinental & Western Air before later becoming known as Trans World Airlines.
TWA is strongly associated with Howard Hughes, who played a major role in its mid-20th-century history.
The Lockheed Constellation became one of the airline's most iconic aircraft and part of its public identity.
American Airlines announced plans in January 2001 to acquire most of TWA's assets.
TWA ceased to exist as an independent airline in 2001.
cultural_memory: TWA is remembered as one of the most glamorous airlines of the Jet Age.
For many travelers, the name brings up images of stylish cabin crews, international travel, and mid-century airport design.
The TWA Flight Center at JFK remains one of the strongest visual symbols of the airline's legacy.
The airline is also remembered for its long association with Howard Hughes and the romance of early long-distance air travel.
overview_md:
**TWA** stood for Trans World Airlines. It was one of the most famous airlines in the United States for most of the 20th century.

It started in 1930 under the name Transcontinental and Western Air. The airline flew passengers across the country and later expanded to international routes, including flights across the Atlantic to Europe.

One of the biggest names connected to TWA is Howard Hughes. He was a wealthy businessman and aviation enthusiast who played a big role in shaping the airline during the 1940s and 1950s. Under his influence, TWA began flying one of the most famous planes of that era, the Lockheed Constellation. The Constellation was known for its unusual curved shape and triple tail fins. It became a symbol of what air travel could look and feel like.

In 1962, TWA opened a new terminal at JFK Airport in New York. The building was designed by architect Eero Saarinen and looked like nothing else at the airport. The sweeping roof and open interior made it one of the most talked-about buildings of its time. The terminal sat empty for years after TWA shut down but reopened in 2019 as the TWA Hotel.

TWA was one of the glamorous airlines of the Jet Age. Flying in the 1950s and 1960s was an event. People dressed up for flights. TWA leaned into that image with stylish cabin design and a strong brand.

By the 1990s the airline was struggling with debt and had a hard time competing with larger rivals. TWA went bankrupt. In January 2001, American Airlines announced it would buy most of TWA's assets. The deal closed later that year and TWA stopped flying as its own airline.

The TWA Hotel, built inside the old Saarinen terminal at JFK, is the main thing that keeps the brand visible today.

timeline.text: Transcontinental and Western Air is formed through the merger of Western Air Express and Transcontinental Air Transport. It starts flying passengers across the country.
The airline grows into a major coast-to-coast carrier and builds a well-known public image.
Howard Hughes shapes the airline's direction during this period. TWA flies the Lockheed Constellation, one of the most famous passenger planes of the era.
TWA becomes one of the main US airlines flying international Jet Age routes, including transatlantic service to Europe.
The TWA Flight Center opens at JFK Airport. Designed by architect Eero Saarinen, it becomes one of the most famous airport buildings ever built.
TWA struggles with debt and heavy competition. The airline goes through bankruptcy and has a hard time recovering as a smaller player in a tough market.
American Airlines announces in January that it will buy most of TWA's assets. TWA stops flying as its own airline later that year.
The old TWA Flight Center at JFK reopens as the TWA Hotel, keeping the airline's name and design legacy alive.

faq.question: What did TWA stand for?
faq.answer: TWA stood for Trans World Airlines. It started in 1930 as Transcontinental and Western Air and later took on the Trans World Airlines name.

faq.question: When did TWA end?
faq.answer: TWA stopped flying as its own airline in 2001 after American Airlines bought most of its assets.

faq.question: Why did TWA fail?
faq.answer: TWA built up a lot of debt over the years and had a hard time competing with larger airlines that had more money and more routes. By 2001 the company was in bankruptcy and American Airlines stepped in to buy most of what was left.

faq.question: Was Howard Hughes involved with TWA?
faq.answer: Yes. Howard Hughes was closely connected to TWA and helped shape one of the most well-known periods in the airline's history during the 1940s and 1950s.

faq.question: What is the TWA Hotel?
faq.answer: The TWA Hotel is a hotel built inside the former TWA Flight Center at JFK Airport. The original terminal was designed by architect Eero Saarinen and opened in 1962. It reopened as a hotel in 2019.
